focus:
Focus on database systems, data models, storage, indexing, query processing, and retrieval techniques for managing and querying multi-modal data (e.g., text, images, audio, video, tables, graphs, sensor data), rather than general multi-modal machine learning or perception models without a clear database/data management contribution.

Included Content (In Scope)

Multi-modal database systems and architectures
Data models for representing and organizing heterogeneous modalities
Storage, compression, and physical layout techniques for multi-modal data
Indexing methods for cross-modal and modality-specific retrieval in database systems
Query languages, query processing, and optimization for multi-modal queries
Cross-modal retrieval and similarity search when framed as a database or data management problem
Joint management of structured, semi-structured, and unstructured multi-modal data
Transaction, consistency, update, and maintenance issues for multi-modal databases
Benchmarking, evaluation, and datasets for multi-modal database workloads
System support for embeddings and feature storage within multi-modal databases (only when part of a broader multi-modal data management system, not standalone vector search)
Metadata management, schema design, and provenance for multi-modal data repositories
Distributed, cloud, or edge database systems for large-scale multi-modal data management
Open-source systems, platforms, and tools for multi-modal data management and querying

Excluded Content (Out of Scope)

General multi-modal foundation models or vision-language models without a database systems contribution
Pure multi-modal representation learning, pre-training, alignment, or fusion research not tied to database management
Computer vision, speech, audio, or NLP tasks treated purely as modeling problems
Generic information retrieval or recommender systems without a clear database angle
Traditional single-modal database research unless it directly supports multi-modal data management
Data mining or analytics on multi-modal data without emphasis on database infrastructure or query processing
Application papers that merely use multi-modal data but do not study how it is stored, indexed, queried, or managed
Benchmark or dataset papers focused only on model accuracy rather than database workload evaluation
Middleware or pipelines for multi-modal AI that lack substantive database/system design elements
Pure vector search or approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) papers focused solely on single-modality embedding search without multi-modal data management
